name: Wright Patman Congressional
description_short: Congressional Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution offering checking, savings, loans, and investment products with a focus on community service and member education.
description_long:
Congressional Federal Credit Union (CFCU) is a federally chartered credit union that serves members through a range of financial products and services. Founded with a legacy of service and integrity, the organization operates as a member-owned cooperative, distinguishing it from traditional for-profit banks. The institution maintains routing number 254074345 and operates physical locations alongside digital banking channels.

CFCU offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), personal loans, auto loans (new and used), home loans, home equity lines of credit, home equity installment loans, credit cards, and investment services. Current rates advertised include personal loans as low as 7.99% APR, new auto loans at 3.99% APR, used auto at 4.79% APR, home equity installment loans at 5.79% APR, and savings certificates yielding up to 3.50% APY. The institution also provides insurance products and maintains a "Home Advantage" program.

CFCU distinguishes itself through member-centric initiatives including annual scholarship programs (the Carleton C.C. Jenkins Memorial Scholarship offering $5,000 per award), active hiring and community engagement, and emphasis on financial security education. The organization demonstrates commitment to fraud prevention awareness through prominent messaging about scam prevention and secure communication practices. Leadership includes President & CEO Tricia Szurgot and Chief Brand & Transformation Officer Brian Holloway.

As a credit union, CFCU serves members rather than shareholders, which typically translates to competitive rates and member-focused policies. However, membership eligibility requirements may restrict access compared to traditional banks. The institution's specific service area and membership requirements are not detailed in available website content, limiting clarity on geographic reach and eligibility criteria.
services: Online banking with member ID login and password management
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with rates up to 3.50% APY
Auto loans for new vehicles (from 3.99% APR) and used vehicles (from 4.79% APR)
Home purchase loans and home refinancing
Home equity lines of credit (from 6.75% APR)
Fraud prevention and financial security education
Carleton C.C. Jenkins Memorial Scholarship program
pros: Member-owned credit union structure typically offers competitive rates and lower fees than traditional banks
Competitive loan rates: new auto loans from 3.99% APR, personal loans from 7.99% APR
Offers scholarship program ($5,000 awards) demonstrating commitment to member community and education
Comprehensive product suite including personal loans, auto loans, mortgages, investment services, and insurance
Active fraud prevention education and secure communication protocols to protect members
Savings certificates (CDs) up to 3.50% APY competitive with market rates
Physical branch locations combined with online banking access for member convenience
cons: Membership eligibility requirements not clearly specified on website, potentially limiting access compared to traditional banks
Website does not disclose specific service area or geographic limitations
No information provided about minimum account balances, membership fees, or fee schedules
Limited transparency on loan approval requirements and qualification criteria
Website emphasizes security warnings against using email for sensitive information, indicating members must contact by phone/mail for account issues
best_for: Members eligible for credit union membership seeking competitive loan rates and personal service
Borrowers pursuing auto loans or mortgages with preference for member-owned financial institutions
Individuals prioritizing fraud protection awareness and security-conscious banking practices
High school seniors and graduating members interested in educational scholarships and community-focused financial institutions
diagnosis: Congressional Federal Credit Union is best for individuals eligible for membership seeking a member-owned alternative to traditional banks with competitive loan rates and a community-focused approach. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility requirements are not publicly specified on the website, so prospective members must verify they qualify before applying, and geographic service areas are not disclosed.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
